name: Buildium API
what_it_does: Property management REST API with OpenAPI spec covering properties, tenants, leases, work orders, and financials for small-to-medium landlords.
use_cases: Automate rent collection reminders and delinquency workflows
Sync property listings and vacancy data with listing platforms
Create and update work orders from maintenance request systems
Export financial reports to accounting software
Onboard new tenants and generate lease documents automatically
not_for: Enterprise portfolios with 10,000+ units (Yardi or RealPage scale better)
Commercial real estate with complex CAM or lease abstractions
best_when: Building property management automation for landlords or small property managers with 1–5,000 units already using Buildium
avoid_when: You need enterprise-scale portfolio management or complex commercial lease accounting

auth.notes: API key or HTTP Basic auth; client ID + secret required; no fine-grained scope control

pricing.paid_tiers: Requires Buildium subscription ($50–$460/month depending on unit count); API access included
pricing.notes: No standalone API plan; must be a Buildium subscriber; sandbox available for existing customers

performance.rate_limits: 1000 req/day on lower tiers; higher on premium plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec enables good schema validation; error responses are consistent with field-level detail
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support; agents must implement deduplication logic themselves
known_agent_gotchas: Daily rate limits on lower tiers are very restrictive for bulk operations
No webhooks — agents must poll for state changes
API key scopes not supported; all-or-nothing access
OpenAPI spec available but may not reflect every field in practice

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant; basic auth and API key lack scope granularity; TLS enforced; no OAuth option
